caption: Table 98. Document revision history
rows: Table 1: Device summary table reordered; Updated last paragraph in Section 2: Description; Updated Table 12: Pin assignment and description; Updated Table 68: VREFBUF characteristics; Missing package marking examples added in Section 6: Package information and Figure 42: LQFP32 package marking example corrected; Section : Device marking updated; Updated example in Section 6.12.2: Selecting the product temperature range; Section 6.12: Thermal characteristics - improved UFBGA100, UFBGA64, and LQFP80 Θ values as a result of characterization; JA Updated Section 6.9: LQFP80 package information (9X).

Updated Figure 11 to Figure 8 and Figure 5 to Figure 4: marking of VDDIO2-only supplied pins corrected. I/O type "_s" added to Table 11: Terms and symbols used in Pin assignment and description table and Table 12: Pin assignment and description.

Added HSI48 in features on the cover page and in Figure 1: Block diagram; Updated Table 2: Features and peripheral counts; Updated Table 12: Pin assignment and description (UFBGA80 corrected to LQFP80) in the header; Updated Section : USB full speed (FS) characteristics; Section UCPD characteristics made a sub-section of Section 5.3.25: Characteristics of communication interfaces; Updated Figure 54: LQFP80 - Outline.

Updated cover page figure with packages, Table 2: Features and peripheral counts, Section 1: Introduction, Section 3.5: Boot modes, Section 3.7.1: Power supply schemes, Section 3.7.4: Low-power modes, Section 3.14: Analog-to- digital converter (ADC), Section 3.18.1: Advanced-control timer (TIM1), Section 3.24: Universal serial bus full-speed host/device interface (USB) (title), Section 4: Pinouts, pin description and alternate functions (packages ordered from lowest to highest pin count, PF2-NRST I/O structure), table footnotes "Guaranteed by design" and "Guaranteed by characterization", Figure 2, Figure 5 through Figure 18, Table 11: Terms and symbols used in Pin assignment and description table, Table 12: Pin assignment and description, Table 13: Port A alternate function mapping (AF0 to AF7), Figure 15: Power supply scheme, Table 22: Current characteristics, Table 24: General operating conditions, sections I/O static current consumption and I/O dynamic current consumption,Table 51: EMI characteristics, Figure 27: Recommended NRST pin protection, Figure 29: ADC typical connection diagram, Figure 31: SPI timing diagram - slave mode and CPHA = 0, Figure 32: SPI timing diagram - slave mode and CPHA = 1, Figure 33: SPI timing diagram - master mode, Table 55: I/O static characteristics, Table 57: Output voltage characteristics, section Output buffer timing characteristics (title), Table 58: Non-FT_c I/O output timing characteristics (footnote 1), Table 59: FT_c I/O output timing characteristics (footnote 1).

Updated Table 62: Analog switch booster characteristics, Table 63: ADC characteristics (footnote 3, W ), Table 65: ADC accuracy, Table 80: USB LATENCY FS electrical characteristics, Table 81: UCPD operating conditions, and Section 6: Package information. Added Table 59: FT_c I/O output timing characteristics, Figure 36: USART timing diagram in SPI master mode, Figure 37: USART timing diagram in SPI slave mode, and section Section 8: Important security notice.

Updated: Section 1: Introduction, Section 2: Description, Table 3: Access status versus readout protection level and execution modes, Table 7: Timer feature comparison, Figure 15: Power supply scheme, Table 21: Voltage characteristics, Table 22: Current characteristics, Table 63: ADC characteristics, Table 68: VREFBUF characteristics, Table 69: COMP characteristics, Figure 24: I/O input characteristics, Section 6.2: LQFP32 package information (5V), Section 6.3: UFQFPN32 package information (A0B8), Section 6.6: WLCSP52 package information (B0BG), Section 6.9: LQFP80 package information (9X), Section 6.10: LQFP100 package information (1L), Section 6.11: UFBGA100 package information (A0C2), Table 97: Package thermal characteristics. Added Section 5.3.16: Extended interrupt and event controller input (EXTI) characteristics.
